Japan (modern borders) — sake's origin + sole traditional producer
description: Modern political border. Nihonshu (sake) has been brewed in Japan for 2,000+ years. Japan remains the overwhelming origin — over 99% of authentic sake production happens here.

Niigata Prefecture (modern borders) — #1 sake producer by volume
description: Modern prefecture border. Niigata produces the most sake by volume + is famous for tanrei-karakuchi (light-dry) style. Cold winters + pure snow-melt water + Gohyakumangoku rice variety.

Hyogo Prefecture / Nada district (modern borders) — #2 sake producer
description: Modern prefecture border. Nada's Miyamizu hard water creates bold koji-forward style; Edo-period Taru Kaisen sake ships departed from here to Edo (Tokyo).

Kyoto Prefecture / Fushimi district (modern borders) — #3 producer
description: Modern prefecture border. Fushimi's softer water produces softer, fruitier style. Imperial-court brewing tradition dates to the Engi-shiki (927 CE).
